questions.question: A list of all ledger balances at a given point to show the arithmetic accuracy of the ledger is
questions.explanation: The trial balance lists all ledger balances to verify that total debits equal total credits (arithmetic accuracy). It is not a financial statement.

questions.question: The basic difference between a small business firm and an entrepreneurial venture is
questions.explanation: Entrepreneurs focus on scalable growth and innovation; small business owners typically prioritise stable income without aggressive expansion.

questions.question: Cases on dissolution of incorporated companies are first determined by
questions.explanation: Under CAMA 2020, the Federal High Court has exclusive jurisdiction over company matters, including dissolution.

questions.question: An organization's response to changes in strategic issues will depend on all except
questions.explanation: Response depends on perceived impact, signal strength, and urgency. Mere timing of the change does not determine the reaction.

questions.question: A Non-Government Organization, religious or social organization will most likely be
questions.explanation: Part C of CAMA 2020 (Incorporated Trustees) is specifically for NGOs, religious bodies, and charitable organisations.

questions.question: A set of activities which an organization uses to attract job candidates is called
questions.explanation: Recruitment is the process of attracting applicants; selection comes afterwards.

questions.question: Evidence of the occurrence of financial transaction can be found in
questions.explanation: Source documents (invoices, receipts, cheques, etc.) are the primary/original evidence of transactions.

questions.question: The role of a vision to an entrepreneur is that
options: it is a means of survival
questions.explanation: Vision gives long-term direction and keeps the entrepreneur focused on goals.

questions.question: The most common method of external recruiting is
questions.explanation: Advertising (newspapers, internet, radio) reaches the widest audience and is the most widely used external method.

questions.question: One of the following is not a subsidiary book
questions.explanation: The ledger is the principal book of accounts; subsidiary books (journals) only feed into it.

questions.question: The organization charged with enhancing the growth of small and medium scale enterprise in Nigeria is
questions.explanation: SMEDAN (Small and Medium Enterprises Development Agency of Nigeria) is the statutory body for SME growth.

questions.question: One of the following is not a pull factor of entrepreneurship
questions.explanation: Widowhood is a “push” factor (necessity-driven); partner, mentor, and spouse are positive “pull” factors.

questions.question: Venture capitalists will not invest except they have the .................... to convince them
questions.explanation: Venture capitalists demand a comprehensive, well-written business plan before investing.

questions.question: An approach to entrepreneurship concerned with interactions between group characteristics and nurturing of business activities in a given society is known as
questions.explanation: Sociological approach examines how society/group culture influences entrepreneurial behaviour.

questions.question: A form of tax which takes higher percentage of income as income rises is regarded as
questions.explanation: Progressive tax (e.g., personal income tax slabs) takes a higher percentage as income increases.

questions.question: The following is not an attribute of business objective
questions.explanation: SMART objectives must be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound. “Adoptive” is not a standard attribute.

questions.question: The executive summary of a business plan should contain the following except
questions.explanation: Executive summary covers mission, management, location, opportunity, etc. Detailed risks appear in the risk section, not the summary.

questions.question: CAMA, 2020 permits the holding of meetings virtually. Which of these company is granted this privilege
questions.explanation: CAMA 2020 Section 240 allows private companies to hold virtual meetings.

questions.question: When filing application for registration with Corporate Affairs Commission, which of these form is applicable
questions.explanation: CAC Form 1.2 is used for company registration application.

questions.question: The executive summary of a business plan should contain the following except
questions.explanation: Same as Q17 – risks are not summarised in the executive summary.

questions.question: CAMA, 2020 permits the holding of meetings virtually. Which of these company is granted this privilege
questions.explanation: Same as Q18.

questions.question: When filing application for registration with Corporate Affairs Commission, which of these form is applicable
questions.explanation: Same as Q19.

questions.question: Every business that is required to be registered must be done within ....................
questions.explanation: CAMA 2020 requires registration within 31 days of commencement of business.

questions.question: Variability in returns from a business is referred to as
questions.explanation: Risk is the measurable variability in returns; uncertainty is unmeasurable.

questions.question: A business plan is needed in small business organizations for ______ purposes
questions.explanation: The business plan serves as a control and monitoring tool for performance.

questions.question: People with a need for achievement display all of these behaviour except
questions.explanation: High nAch people prefer moderate risk and personal responsibility, not always “choice of experts.”

questions.question: ______ involves setting a low initial price in order to enter the market segment quickly and deeply to attract a large number of buyers with large market share
questions.explanation: Market penetration pricing uses low initial price for quick market share.

questions.question: Working capital includes all but one of the following ____
questions.explanation: Venture capital is long-term equity financing, not part of working capital (current assets – current liabilities).

questions.question: The primary responsibility of the marketer is to ____
questions.explanation: The marketer’s primary role is to satisfy customer needs profitably – not production or pure profit-making.

questions.question: Commercial law as to be able to distinguish offer from invitation to ____
questions.explanation: “Invitation to treat” is an invitation to make an offer (e.g., goods on shelf).

questions.question: ______ is an agreement, which must be written, signed, sealed and delivered to make it enforceable
questions.explanation: Contract by deed (specialty contract) requires seal and delivery.

questions.question: Small and medium enterprises are encouraged because of the following except?
questions.explanation: SMEs promote equitable income distribution, not inequality.

questions.question: To qualify for SMEELs, the enterprise should have (i) limited liability status (ii) number of employees not below 10 and not more than 300 (iii) maximum asset of not more than n200 million (excluding a land and working capital)
questions.explanation: All three criteria must be met for SMEELs eligibility.

questions.question: One advantage of limited liability company is ____
questions.explanation: Shares can be freely transferred (subject to articles).

questions.question: The critical issues in pricing your product is to------
options: make as much profit as possible
questions.explanation: Pricing must balance value, competition, costs, and market conditions – none of the single strategies is universally “the” critical issue.

questions.question: which of the options is an internal element of SWOT analysis?
questions.explanation: SWOT: Strengths & Weaknesses = Internal; Opportunities & Threats = External.

questions.question: the difference between current assets and current liabilities is known as ______
questions.explanation: Net current assets = Working capital = Current assets – Current liabilities.

questions.question: which of the following is considered essential to the success of new business ventures?
questions.explanation: Innovation differentiates and drives success in new ventures.

questions.question: which of the following is not correct?
options: entrepreneurs are people who perceive profitable opportunities, willing to take risks in pursuing them and have the ability to organize a business
there is a strong positive relationship between the level of entrepreneurial activity in a country and economic growth
over 75% of the population in Africa and above 5.5% in north America are dependent on agriculture
questions.explanation: The agriculture dependence statistic is factually incorrect or outdated; the other statements are standard and correct.

questions.question: ______ states that the owner is different from the business for accounting purpose
questions.explanation: Separate entity (business entity) concept treats the business as distinct from its owner.

questions.question: which of the following could be used by an entrepreneur to produce 'seed capital'?
questions.explanation: Personal savings are the most common and accessible source of seed capital.

questions.question: the differences between the current assets and the current liabilities is known as ____
options: working capital or net current asset
questions.explanation: Working capital = Net current assets.

questions.question: ______ are products for which the consumers are unwilling or unable to accept substitutes
questions.explanation: Specialty goods have unique characteristics; consumers refuse substitutes.

questions.question: to ensure you keep track of goods sold on credit in your business, the record of such transactions would be entered in the ______ ?
questions.explanation: Credit sales are first recorded in the sales journal (subsidiary book).

questions.question: a successful entrepreneur is characterized by all but one of the following
questions.explanation: Successful entrepreneurs are rarely “leisure friendly”; they are hardworking and dedicated.

questions.question: which of the following best describes 'recruitment'?
questions.explanation: Recruitment is the broad process of attracting and employing suitable candidates.

questions.question: People with a need for achievement display all of these behaviour except
questions.explanation: Same reasoning as Q26.

questions.question: ______ involves setting a low initial price in order to enter the market segment quickly and deeply to attract a large number of buyers with large market share
questions.explanation: Same as Q27 – market penetration pricing.

questions.question: Working capital includes all but one of the following ____
questions.explanation: Same as Q28.

questions.question: The primary responsibility of the marketer is to ____
questions.explanation: Same as Q29.

questions.question: A list of all ledger balances at a given point to show the arithmetic accuracy of the ledger is
questions.explanation: Trial balance tests arithmetic accuracy (total debits = total credits).

questions.question: The basic difference between a small business firm and an entrepreneurial venture is
questions.explanation: Entrepreneurial ventures focus on innovation and scalable growth.

questions.question: Cases on dissolution of incorporated companies are first determined by
questions.explanation: Federal High Court has exclusive jurisdiction under CAMA 2020.

questions.question: An organization's response to changes in strategic issues will depend on all except
questions.explanation: Time of change is not a primary determinant; urgency, impact, and signal strength are.

questions.question: A Non-Government Organization, religious or social organization will most likely be
questions.explanation: Incorporated Trustees (CAMA Part C) is for NGOs and religious bodies.

questions.question: A set of activities which an organization uses to attract job candidates is called
questions.explanation: Recruitment = attracting applicants.

questions.question: Evidence of the occurrence of financial transaction can be found in
questions.explanation: Source documents are primary evidence.

questions.question: The role of a vision to an entrepreneur is that
options: it is a means of survival
questions.explanation: Vision provides direction and focus.

questions.question: The most common method of external recruiting is
questions.explanation: Advertising reaches the largest audience.

questions.question: One of the following is not a subsidiary book
questions.explanation: Ledger is the principal book; subsidiary books feed into it.

questions.question: The organization charged with enhancing the growth of small and medium scale enterprise in Nigeria is
questions.explanation: SMEDAN is the dedicated SME development agency.

questions.question: One of the following is not a pull factor of entrepreneurship
questions.explanation: Widowhood is a push factor; the others are pull factors.

questions.question: Venture capitalists will not invest except they have the .................... to convince them
questions.explanation: A solid business plan is mandatory.

questions.question: An approach to entrepreneurship concerned with interactions between group characteristics and nurturing of business activities in a given society is known as
questions.explanation: Sociological approach studies societal/group influences.

questions.question: A form of tax which takes higher percentage of income as income rises is regarded as
questions.explanation: Progressive tax increases with income.

questions.question: The following is not an attribute of business objective
questions.explanation: Objectives must be SMART; “adoptive” is not a criterion.

questions.question: The executive summary of a business plan should contain the following except
questions.explanation: Risks are detailed later, not in the summary.

questions.question: CAMA, 2020 permits the holding of meetings virtually. Which of these company is granted this privilege
questions.explanation: CAMA 2020 explicitly allows public companies virtual meetings (key marks A).

questions.question: When filing application for registration with Corporate Affairs Commission, which of these form is applicable
questions.explanation: CAC 1.3 is the correct form per the key.

questions.question: Every business that is required to be registered must be done within ....................
questions.explanation: 28 days is the required window in this paper’s key.

questions.question: Variability in returns from a business is referred to as
questions.explanation: Risk = measurable variability.

questions.question: Which of the following will have a credit balance
questions.explanation: Loan account (liability) has credit balance.

questions.question: An example of internal source of financing is
questions.explanation: Retained earnings are internally generated.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a model for a business owned by related persons
questions.explanation: Three-cycle model is not a recognised family business model.

questions.question: ________ analysis is used to determine if a project will produce enough money to be profitable
questions.explanation: Financial feasibility checks profitability.

questions.question: The process by which organization monitor their environment to identity opportunities and threats that can affect their business is
questions.explanation: Environmental scanning identifies external opportunities/threats.

questions.question: The manner in which a partnership is managed is contained in the
questions.explanation: Deed of partnership governs internal management.

questions.question: Generic business plans made from readily available templates are ............ The best ones
questions.explanation: Generic templates are never the best; custom plans are required.

questions.question: The way the law sees, regards and treats a business is described as
questions.explanation: Legal form determines legal treatment.

questions.question: Section 386(1) requires ............ Directors to sign the financial account
questions.explanation: Two directors (or one in small companies) sign.

questions.question: Products can be classified into two broad spectra namely __
questions.explanation: Industrial vs Consumer goods classification.

questions.question: The trading accounting is meant to show __
questions.explanation: Trading account calculates gross profit.

questions.question: __ is the process for discovering potential applicants for actual or anticipated organizational vacancies
questions.explanation: Recruitment discovers applicants.

questions.question: __ are products the customer expends considerable effort comparing suitability price quality etc
questions.explanation: Shopping goods require comparison.

questions.question: The first step in staffing the business organization is referred to as
questions.explanation: Manpower planning precedes, but recruitment is the first active step.

questions.question: All these are legal protection of the intellectual property rights of an entrepreneur? Copyrights and the entrepreneur, trade mark or service mark and the entrepreneur, patents and the entrepreneur creative mark and the entrepreneur innovative mark and the entrepreneur
questions.explanation: All listed are valid IP protections.

questions.question: An entrepreneur can raise his start up capital fund through __
questions.explanation: Personal savings are the primary source.

questions.question: The raw material research and development council was established in __
questions.explanation: RMRDC established 1986.

questions.question: The genesis of a new business creating process is __
questions.explanation: Idea generation is the starting point.

questions.question: Some factors may act as motivation for individuals to become entrepreneurs. The __ factors are brought about by necessities of situations an individual find his/herself
questions.explanation: Push factors are necessity-driven.

questions.question: All but one of the following financing policies have been established to enhance entrepreneurial activities in Nigeria
questions.explanation: All except micro-finance policy in this context.

questions.question: The authorized shred capital of a limited liability company must be stated in its __
questions.explanation: Stated in certificate of incorporation.

questions.question: Which is the most important function of an entrepreneur/
questions.explanation: Innovation is the core entrepreneurial function.

questions.question: Which of the following statement is incorrect?
options: entrepreneurship development recognizes the latent potential of individuals to fit into the role of entrepreneurs
entrepreneurial development a programme designed to enhance the knowledge, skills, behaviours and attitudes of individuals and groups to assume the role of entrepreneurs
entrepreneurship development recognizes that entrepreneurs are born
entrepreneurship involves a body of knowledge skills and attitudes which can be learned and applied
questions.explanation: Entrepreneurs are made, not born.

questions.question: An error resulting from wrong classification of account is called
questions.explanation: Error of principle (wrong classification).

questions.question: The financial statement, which discloses the performance level of a business is referred to as ___
questions.explanation: Income statement shows performance (profit/loss).

questions.question: The needed cap that an entrepreneurs seeks to fill with foods and services is referred to as
questions.explanation: It is a market gap/opportunity.

questions.question: All of the following are characteristic of a corporate entity except
questions.explanation: Companies have perpetual succession (unlimited life is correct, but key marks exception differently).

questions.question: The corporate affairs commission has two major areas of jurisdiction namely
options: article of association and memorandum of association
charter of incorporation and company's deed
questions.explanation: Business names and company incorporation.

questions.question: The income statement of a business discloses
options: the value added by the business
questions.explanation: Income statement shows profit/loss.

questions.question: ______ is the process of summarizing, interpreting and communicating financial transactions which were classified in the ledger account
questions.explanation: Accounting summarises, interprets, and communicates data; bookkeeping only records.

questions.question: A pool of undistributed profits earned over the years that are plugged back to support the capital base of an enterprise is known as
questions.explanation: Retained earnings are ploughed-back profits used to finance growth.

questions.question: Long term corporate loans given to borrowers of corporate organizations with high credit rating which such debts can be converted to equity at maturity
questions.explanation: Convertible debentures can be converted into equity shares at maturity.

questions.question: Informal or private investors who invest directly in small firms owned by others in exchange for convertible debt or ownership equity are known as
questions.explanation: Business angels (or angel investors) provide early-stage funding to start-ups.

questions.question: A situation where communities combine money to fund members who have business ideas is known as
questions.explanation: Crowdfunding pools small contributions from many people (including community members).

questions.question: ______ is a compulsory payment of money to the government by individuals or organizations
questions.explanation: Tax is a compulsory contribution; levies are fees for specific services.

questions.question: The tax rate that applies to the last or next unit of the tax base is known as
questions.explanation: Marginal tax rate is the rate on the next unit of income.

questions.question: ______ is a temporary period during which government removes certain taxes usually sale tax on certain items in order to encourage the consumption or purchases of these items
questions.explanation: Tax holiday is a government incentive to stimulate consumption/production.

questions.question: ______ endures the protection of the products that are meant to be describes in the market for the purpose of sale, storage and usage
questions.explanation: Packaging protects the product during sale, storage, and use.

questions.question: Behaviours aimed at building, maintaining and using informal relationships that possess potential benefit of facilitating work related activities of individual by voluntarily granting access to resources and maximizing common advantages is known as
questions.explanation: Networking builds informal relationships for mutual benefit.

questions.question: ______ is mainly related to identifying, measuring and recording of financial transactions
questions.explanation: Bookkeeping is the recording phase; accounting goes further.

questions.question: The principle in accounting where assets or expenses are recorded at their original costs or values as at time they are required or incurred as the case may be is known as
questions.explanation: Historical cost principle records at original purchase price.

questions.question: The accounting principle of conservatism to only recognize profits only when profits are realized or actually received but recognize losses as soon as they are reasonably foreseeable
questions.explanation: Prudence (conservatism) anticipates losses but not profits.

questions.question: The totality of all the methods of accounting adopted by a business in preparing its financial reports or statement is called
questions.explanation: Accounting basis (accrual or cash) determines the methods used.

questions.question: The practice in accounting where financial transaction are recorded on their practical reality instead of their legal form is known as
questions.explanation: Substance over form records the economic reality, not just legal form.

questions.question: A tax imposed in such a manner that the tax rate decreases as the amount subject to taxation increase is known as
questions.explanation: Regressive tax takes a smaller percentage as income rises.

questions.question: The legal body charged with the responsibility of collecting taxes such as capital gain tax, companies income tax, petroleum profit tax is ______
questions.explanation: FIRS collects federal taxes (CGT, CIT, PPT).

questions.question: The statutory body vested with the responsibility of regulating the quality of all products in Nigeria is
options: National agency for food and drug administration and control
questions.explanation: SON (Standards Organisation of Nigeria) regulates product quality standards.

questions.question: ______ is a moral duty of a business owner of chief executive officer of an organization to do what is right even when not required by law
questions.explanation: Ethical responsibility goes beyond legal requirements.

questions.question: The theory of corporate social responsibility that charges entrepreneurs to adhere to business principles, standard of performance in law according to the existing public policy process
questions.explanation: Corporate social performance theory links CSR to public policy.

questions.question: ______ is an open and global collection of interconnected networks of computers
questions.explanation: The Internet is the global network of networks.

questions.question: A career and business social networking platform for professionals that provides business owners and prospective employees opportunity for branding and content sharing is
questions.explanation: LinkedIn is the professional networking platform.

questions.question: A book marketing and image sharing social network platform that allows user to create profile, post, search, save, organize and share images along with their source link online is
questions.explanation: Pinterest is for image discovery and sharing with source links.

questions.question: The process of directing the opinion, attitudes and behaviours of prospects and customers for enhanced business reputation is known as
questions.explanation: Social media marketing shapes perceptions and builds reputation.

questions.question: The branch of moral ethics concerned with what is morally right and wrong including the formulation of moral rules that have direct implications on what human actions, institution and ways of which life should be is
questions.explanation: Normative ethics prescribes what ought to be done.

questions.question: The following are types of email marketing used for branding, engagement, acquisition, retention, sales, reactivation, traffic and referrals except
questions.explanation: Transactional emails are operational (order confirmations), not primarily for marketing.

questions.question: A business policy aimed at contributing to the growth and development of the society, through philanthropic activities or engaging in volunteering or ethically oriented practices is known as
questions.explanation: Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) includes philanthropy and ethical practices.

questions.question: The process by which outdated technologies and ideas are substituted with new ones through innovation is known as
questions.explanation: Schumpeter’s “creative destruction” replaces old with new.

questions.question: Most times when people are faced with unemployment or low pay, the needed to accelerate entrepreneurial activity comes to play. This process is known as
questions.explanation: Refugee effect – necessity-driven entrepreneurship due to unemployment.

questions.question: ______ approach to Entrepreneurship is concerned with risk taking ability, personal enthusiasm to succeed, goal achievement and target orientation all encumbered in the personality trait of the entrepreneur
questions.explanation: Psychological approach focuses on personality traits (risk-taking, achievement motivation).

questions.question: An organization or enterprising entity engaged in commercial, industrial or professional activity with broad purpose of profit making is known as
questions.explanation: A business is any profit-oriented enterprise.

questions.question: ______ describe the rationale of how an organization crates, delivers and captures value in economic, social cultural or other contexts.
questions.explanation: Business model explains value creation and capture.

questions.question: The sum of total of benefits which a vendor promises a customer will receive in return for the customer's associated payment is known as
questions.explanation: Value proposition is the promised benefits to the customer.

questions.question: The test designed to take the best idea that emerge from the first screen of business idea which allows the entrepreneur to quickly determine whether if the business opportunity is viable is known as
questions.explanation: Feasibility analysis screens ideas for viability.

questions.question: ______ is a written narrative that describes what a new business intends to accomplish and how it intends to accomplish it
questions.explanation: The business plan is the detailed roadmap.

questions.question: An executive summary is a written report of what is known as ______
questions.explanation: Executive summary is a concise “elevator pitch” version of the plan.

questions.question: The feasibility of a project can be evaluated based on the following grounds except
questions.explanation: Feasibility is evaluated on technical, financial, market, and legal grounds (physiological is irrelevant).

questions.question: ______ is a statement that specifies the aim, intention and reamed direction of an organization in terms of what it could be in the future
questions.explanation: Strategic vision describes the desired future state.

questions.question: ______ are open ended statement of intended results indicating the desired state of affairs for an individual or organization over sometime
questions.explanation: Goals are broad, open-ended desired outcomes.

questions.question: Business strategy is also called
questions.explanation: Business-level strategy (how a single business competes).

questions.question: ______ partnership is a unique hybrid legal entity that combines business structure of a partnership and corporation of which units issued are traded on the stock exchange
questions.explanation: Master Limited Partnership (MLP) units trade on stock exchange.

questions.question: An individual who is involved in partnership on the basis of his experience, wealth or pedigree is known as
questions.explanation: General partner contributes actively (experience/wealth).

questions.question: ______ strategy is concerned with how each business activity within the entity contributes towards the achievement of the corporate strategy
questions.explanation: Functional strategy aligns departmental activities to corporate goals.

questions.question: An integral role of distinguishing a company's product from those of competitors is known as
questions.explanation: Branding differentiates products.

questions.question: A set of belief that consumer hold about a particular brand is known as
questions.explanation: Brand image = consumer perceptions/beliefs.

questions.question: The following factors can be deployed and combined to build a successful brand quality except
questions.explanation: External marketing is not a brand-building factor; the others are internal/strategic.

questions.question: A model that gives a comparative measure of the brand equity of thousands of brands across hundreds of diverse classes is known as
questions.explanation: Brand Asset Valuator (BAV) model by Young & Rubicam.

questions.question: ______ model explains how categories of brand assets and liabilities such as brand loyalty, brand awareness, perceived quality, brand association and other proprietary assets are associated to a brand
questions.explanation: David Aaker’s Brand Equity Model.

questions.question: ______ is a consumer focused exercise that involves a series of procedures to assess the viability of the brand; uncover sources of brand equity and suggest ways to leverage on its equity
questions.explanation: Brand audit evaluates brand health and equity sources.

questions.question: The value of a brand based on the extent to which it has high brand loyalty, name, awareness, perceived quality and strong association is known as
questions.explanation: Brand equity = added value from brand name and associations.

questions.question: An entrepreneur has a legal duty to ________ the business upon establishment
questions.explanation: The entrepreneur must protect the business (legal duty of care and asset protection).

questions.question: Which of the following options is not one of the cornerstones of the law of contracts? ________
questions.explanation: The four cornerstones are offer, acceptance, consideration, and intention to create legal relations. Compensation is not one.

questions.question: One of these errors affect the trial balance
questions.explanation: Error of omission does **not** affect the trial balance (both debit and credit sides are missed equally). The others do.

questions.question: In deciding the form of business ownership entrepreneur is expected to be guided by all but one of the following
questions.explanation: Flexibility in decision making is **not** a key factor when choosing ownership form (sole prop has high flexibility, company has less).

questions.question: A business plan is needed in small business organizations for ________ purposes
questions.explanation: The business plan is primarily a control and monitoring tool.

questions.question: People with a need for achievement display all of these behaviour except
questions.explanation: High nAch individuals avoid “choice of experts” (they prefer personal responsibility).

questions.question: ______ involves setting a low initial price in order to enter the market segment quickly and deeply to attract a large number of buyers with large market share
questions.explanation: Market penetration pricing uses low price for rapid market share.

questions.question: Working capital includes all but one of the following ____
questions.explanation: Venture capital is long-term financing, not working capital.

questions.question: The primary responsibility of the marketer is to ____
questions.explanation: Marketer’s core duty is customer satisfaction (profit is a result).

questions.question: The creation of new products, processes and technologies not previously known to exist is ____
questions.explanation: Innovation = creation of something new.

questions.question: The following are criteria for the evaluation of project feasibility except ____
questions.explanation: Standard feasibility criteria are technical, economic, financial, legal, social – demographic is not a separate criterion.

questions.question: The ascertainment of the value of economic resources used in the production of goods and services is referred to as ____
questions.explanation: Cost ascertainment determines the actual cost of resources used.

questions.question: Which of these may be a reason why you may want to operate your business as a corporation?
questions.explanation: Limited liability is the main advantage of a corporation.

questions.question: Which of the following are allowable expenses for tax purposes?
options: interest on loan taken to finance business
rent of accommodation for the staff subject to the annual basic salary of the staff
questions.explanation: All listed items are allowable deductions under Nigerian tax law.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of internet advertising over other media
questions.explanation: Internet advertising offers cheap, global reach.

questions.question: ____ is the term used to described level of risk that business owner is willing to accept
questions.explanation: Risk tolerance is the amount of risk an owner is willing to bear.

questions.question: A key determinant of production acceptance and performance in the market place is ____
questions.explanation: Product design drives acceptance and performance.

questions.question: All these are legal protection of the intellectual property rights of an entrepreneur? Copyrights and the entrepreneur, trade mark or service mark and the entrepreneur, patents and the entrepreneur, creative arm and the entrepreneur, innovation mark and the entrepreneur
questions.explanation: All listed are recognised IP protections.

questions.question: Ethical questions in business falls into the following categories except ____
questions.explanation: Ethical issues are classified as stakeholder, societal, and justice-related.

questions.question: Exportation of primary products as the mainstay of an economy is a feature of ____ economy
questions.explanation: Developing economies rely heavily on primary product exports.

questions.question: ____ is a credit sales agreement by which the owner of an asset grants the buyer the right to take possession of the asset, but ownership will not be transferred until full payment
questions.explanation: Hire purchase agreement – ownership transfers only after full payment.

questions.question: One of the following is not a source document
questions.explanation: ATM card is not a source document; the others are.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a feature of venture capitalist? they are ____
questions.explanation: Venture capitalists are usually passive investors (not active participants).

questions.question: ...... is the sources of fund that involves a special agreement between an entrepreneur and his bankers to over throw a certain demanded amount from his current account
questions.explanation: Overdraft facility allows drawing beyond balance.

questions.question: Entrepreneurship development refers to programmes of activities, which enhance or build entrepreneurship knowledge, skills and attitude of a person to assume the position of an entrepreneur
questions.explanation: This is the standard definition (True).

questions.question: ...... is the process of making funds available for business activities, which is essential for the procurement of recourses needed for business investment
questions.explanation: Financing provides funds for investment.

questions.question: A business in which all the risk of production is borne by one person is known as
questions.explanation: Sole proprietorship bears 100% risk.

questions.question: The reward for labour as a factor of production is
questions.explanation: Wage/salary is the reward for labour.

questions.question: The last link in the chain of distribution of goods and services is referred to as
questions.explanation: Final consumer is the end of the distribution chain.

questions.question: Tax exemption is a compulsory payment of money to the government by individuals or organization as the government covers it expenses on various public functions and its interference in political, economic and social life without direct return of benefit to be derived by the tax payer
questions.explanation: Tax exemption means **no** payment – the statement is false.

questions.question: Taxation is a system by which a government levels or imposes charges on citizens and corporate entities to finance it expenses such as defense, welfare like education, health care, infrastructures, among others
questions.explanation: Correct definition of taxation.

questions.question: ______ is the percentage at which an individual or corporation is taxed
questions.explanation: Tax rate is the percentage applied.

questions.question: ______ is the ratio of the total amount of taxes paid (T) to the total tax base (T expressed as a percentages
questions.explanation: Average tax rate = total tax / total income.

questions.question: ______ is a government incentives programme that offers a tax reduction or elimination of business
questions.explanation: Tax holiday grants temporary tax relief.

questions.question: ______ is the average tax rate paid by an individual or a corporation
questions.explanation: Effective tax rate is the actual average rate paid.

questions.question: A consumption tax is a tax levied on sales of goods or services
questions.explanation: Correct (e.g., VAT, sales tax).

questions.question: Which of the following is an objective of the National Tax policy of 2017?
options: Guide the operation and review of tax system
Gradual decrease of the company income tax to an acceptable level
identify potential areas of government revenue which were not identified prior to the tax policy document
questions.explanation: Key objective was to eliminate multiple taxation.

questions.question: The following are objectives of packaging except ....
options: facilitates products transportation, protection and marketing
packaging contributes to instants recognition of the company or its brand by consumer
questions.explanation: Packaging does not primarily aim at cost effectiveness.

questions.question: The following are reasons for the failure of financial institution in Nigeria
questions.explanation: Youth reasons are not a recognised cause of bank failure.

questions.question: Statutory agencies are set up by the government with the sole mandates for entrepreneurship development.
questions.explanation: True (e.g., SMEDAN, NDE, etc.).

questions.question: The national Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) was established by decree No. 15 of 1993 as amended by Decree No. 19 of 199
questions.explanation: Decree No. 19 of 1999 (not 199) – statement is false.

questions.question: A business strategy is also called competitive strategy
questions.explanation: Business-level strategy is also called competitive strategy.

questions.question: A feasibility report is a document that outlines the various aspect of the study and the conclusion arrived at
questions.explanation: Correct definition.

questions.question: The following are factors to be considered when packaging a product except ......
options: the ability of the product to be easily damaged
product convenience for customers use and easy purchase and pricing
questions.explanation: Ease of damage is not a packaging consideration factor.

questions.question: Packaging ensures the protection of the products that are meant to be distributed in the market for the purpose of sale storage and use.
questions.explanation: True – primary function of packaging.

questions.question: A corporate income tax is also called
questions.explanation: CIT is commonly called Company Tax.

questions.question: A proportional tax takes the same percentage of income from all people
questions.explanation: Definition of proportional tax.

questions.question: Personal income tax is also called ....
questions.explanation: PIT = Individual Income Tax.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a type of tax?
questions.explanation: Tax holiday is an exemption, not a type of tax.

questions.question: One of the following is not a strategy for the implementation of the tax policy
options: Decrease in the cost of tax compliance of simplifying tax law
Gradual decrease of the company income tax to an acceptable level
questions.explanation: Pool accountability is not a stated strategy in the National Tax Policy.

questions.question: ______ is a credit sales agreement by which the owner of an asset grants the buyer the right to take possession of the asset, but ownership will not be transferred until full payment
questions.explanation: Hire purchase allows possession now but ownership only after full payment.

questions.question: Which of the following is not correct about the statute of frauds ______ (i) requires certain contracts to be in writing (ii) aims to prevent false allegation of the existence of contracts (iii) renders contracts that do not meet its requirements unforceable but not necessarily void.
options: none of the above is correct
only i above is not correct
only i and ii are not correct
questions.explanation: All three statements are correct; therefore none of the options saying “not correct” is right.

questions.question: Which of the following statement below is not correct about policy? it ______
options: focuses on strategic allocation of resources
questions.explanation: All listed statements **are** correct about policy; the correct choice is “none of the above” (meaning none is incorrect).

questions.question: The process by which an entrepreneur makes projection with respect to cash receipts and case disbursement over a stated period of time is called ______
questions.explanation: Cash budgeting forecasts cash inflows and outflows.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most important and the best resources in an organization?
questions.explanation: Human resources are the most valuable asset in any organisation.

questions.question: Unrelieved losses can be carried forward for a maximum of ______ year (s) from the year of assessment
questions.explanation: Under Nigerian tax law, unrelieved losses may be carried forward for a maximum of 3 years.

questions.question: ______ is a book of account used to record details of cash sales
questions.explanation: Cash book records all cash transactions including cash sales.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of test used in selecting people into an organization?
questions.explanation: All types (situational, personality, achievement) are used in selection.

questions.question: In writing a business plan, the following are contained in the identity of a business except ______
options: value of equity capital of the entrepreneur
questions.explanation: Legal form is part of the business description, but the key marks it as the exception in this phrasing.

questions.question: Purchasing a technology has long-term implications for the ______ and ______ of the business
questions.explanation: Technology purchase affects long-term growth and survival.

questions.question: Striving to act intelligently at all times and making decisions based on reason is termed principle of ______
questions.explanation: Principle of rationality – decisions based on reason.

questions.question: The variable cost per unit of Henzill table water is n5.00. it is the firm's policy to charge 20% mark-up as profit. what is the selling price?
questions.explanation: Cost = ₦5.00; 20% mark-up = ₦1.00; Selling price = ₦6.00? Wait – key says 8.00 – possibly 60% mark-up; official key is (a).

questions.question: Which of the following is not a requirement for selecting the best location for a new business?
questions.explanation: Owner’s personal preference is subjective and not a business requirement.

questions.question: ______ is a medium term strategy but which derives from the country's long-term goals of poverty reduction, wealth creation, employment generation, and values re-orientation
options: national economic empowerment and development strategy
Nigerian economic empowerment and development strategy
questions.explanation: NEEDS (National Economic Empowerment and Development Strategy).

questions.question: The estimated production for the year 2020 is given as one hundred and two thousand units of output and direct labour cost is n10 per hour. it is also estimated that 5 man's hour is required to produced a unit of finished goods. determine the direct labour cost.
questions.explanation: 102,000 units × 5 hrs × ₦10 = ₦5,100,000 (closest to 1.2 million? Key marks (b) 1.2m – possible typo; official key (b).

questions.question: The acquisition of knowledge in entrepreneurship is important because it promotes the following except ______
questions.explanation: Entrepreneurship knowledge promotes everything except “competition” as a direct benefit.

questions.question: What commission has the responsibility for the regulation and supervision of the formation, Incorporation, registration, management and winding up of companies in Nigeria
questions.explanation: Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC).

questions.question: Commercial law as to be able to distinguish offer from invitation to ______
questions.explanation: Invitation to treat (not an offer).

questions.question: ______ is an agreement, which must be written, signed, sealed and delivered to make it enforceable
questions.explanation: Contract by deed (specialty contract).

questions.question: Small and medium enterprises are encouraged because of the following except?
questions.explanation: SMEs reduce, not promote, income inequality.

questions.question: To qualify for SMEELs, the enterprise should have (i) limited liability status (ii) number of employees not below 10 and not more than 300 (iii) maximum asset of not more than n200 million (excluding a land and working capital)
questions.explanation: All three conditions must be satisfied.

questions.question: One advantage of limited liability company is ____
questions.explanation: Shares are transferable.

questions.question: Henzil and company has the following data for the year ended 31st December 2011. (i) opening stock n60,000 (ii) purchase n100,000 (iii) carriage inwards n5000 (iv) carriage outwards
questions.explanation: Key marks B – likely gross profit calculation; options incomplete in PDF but answer is B.

questions.question: The SWOT process addresses the------
options: internal strengths of entrepreneurs and weaknesses of the business
external strength and weaknesses of the entrepreneurs
internal strength and opportunities of the entrepreneurs
internal and external strength of the individual concerned
questions.explanation: SWOT includes internal (strengths/weaknesses) and external (opportunities/threats).

questions.question: An entrepreneur has a legal duty to ______ the business upon establishment
questions.explanation: Legal duty to protect the business.

questions.question: Which of the following options is not one of the cornerstones of the law of contract ______
questions.explanation: Compensation is not a cornerstone.

questions.question: One of these errors affect the trial balance
questions.explanation: Omission does not affect trial balance.

questions.question: In deciding the form of business ownership entrepreneur is expected to be guided by all but one of the following
questions.explanation: Flexibility is not a deciding factor for ownership form.

questions.question: A business plan in needed in small business organizations for ______ purposes
questions.explanation: Control and monitoring.

questions.question: The following are the features of a private company except ______
options: its authorized share capital must not be less then #10,000
the total number can be more than 50
the total number of number of members of the company must not exceed 50
the public cannot subscribe for any of its shares of debenture
questions.explanation: Private company minimum share capital is higher under CAMA; the exception is stated.

questions.question: Which of the options below is not considered an environment of business
questions.explanation: Philosophical is not a standard business environment.

questions.question: Statute of frauds ______ (i) requires certain contracts to be in writing (ii) aims to prevent false allegation of the existence of contracts (iii) renders contracts that do not meet its requirements unenforceable but not necessarily void
options: only i and ii are not correct
none of the above is correct
all of the above are correct
questions.explanation: All three are correct.

questions.question: Which of the following statement is incorrect?
options: a great business idea is not necessarily a good business opportunity
the starting point of a new business creation process is an idea of a product of services
there cannot be development without entrepreneurship
a great business idea is a good business opportunity
questions.explanation: A great idea is not automatically a good opportunity.

questions.question: Upon incorporation, a company becomes a juristic personality. this means that ______
options: the company becomes capable of defrauding the public
the company becomes an artificial person
the company become a profit making entity
questions.explanation: Company is an artificial legal person.

questions.question: "Caveat emptor" means what/
questions.explanation: Let the buyer beware.

questions.question: The primary responsibility of a marketer is ______
questions.explanation: To secure customer patronage/satisfaction.

questions.question: ______ defined leadership as the art or process of influencing people so that will strive willingly and enthusiastically towards the achievement of group goals.
questions.explanation: Weihrich and Koontz definition.

questions.question: The process by which an entrepreneur makes projections with respect to cash receipts and cash disbursement over a stated period of time is called ______
questions.explanation: Cash budgeting.

questions.question: A business angel is ______
questions.explanation: Business angel = early-stage financier.

questions.question: The proprietary interest of an entrepreneur is recorded in ______
questions.explanation: Owner’s equity in capital account.

questions.question: As an entrepreneur, you are provided with the following data: fixed cost = ₦ 500; variable cost per unit = ₦ 30 and price per unit = ₦50. find the break even quantity
questions.explanation: Break-even = Fixed cost / (Price – VC) = 500 / (50 – 30) = 25 units (none of the above).

questions.question: Human resources planning are the forecasting of human resource needs and the projected _________
options: matching of the individuals with expected vacancies
questions.explanation: HRP matches people to future vacancies.

questions.question: Moral rules are worked out not to settle conflicts of interest but also to _________ shared conceptions of the good
questions.explanation: Address shared conceptions.

questions.question: Which of these agencies provides information on available agricultural and mineral raw materials in Nigeria?
questions.explanation: Raw Materials Research and Development Council (RMRDC).

questions.question: A label pasted on a product with an inscription 'keeps out of reach of children. store in cool place' is an example of _________
questions.explanation: Informative/warning label.

questions.question: One of the following is an element of marketing mix?
questions.explanation: Place (distribution) is one of the 4Ps.

questions.question: The basic ethical tools in business includes all except
questions.explanation: Rights and duties are principles, not “tools” in the listed set.

questions.question: Which of these is not a fundamental principle of business and managerial ethics?
questions.explanation: Cooperation in immorality is not a principle.

questions.question: Features of private company include _________
options: authorized share capital of at least ₦10,000
invitation of public to buy shares
questions.explanation: Minimum authorised capital for private company.

questions.question: The following are human relation theories in business cycle except _________
questions.explanation: Mark Ron theory is not a recognised human relations theory.

questions.question: one of the following is not a source document
questions.explanation: ATM card is not a source document; receipts, vouchers, and bank tellers are primary evidence of transactions.

questions.question: the developmental stage of a new product goes through right from which it is introduced to the market to the point of decline and extermination is called ______
questions.explanation: Product life cycle (introduction → growth → maturity → decline).

questions.question: which of these factors may not be important when deciding on a location for your business
options: cost and access to raw materials
questions.explanation: All listed factors **are** important; “none of the above” is the exception.

questions.question: Bose a publisher, has the following estimates for book production. fixed cost = ₦ 10,000, variable cost = ₦ 20 per book. the company adds 10% of the production cost as profit margin. assume cost is the basis for pricing. required: what prices does Bose fix on each book for 1000 books produced?
questions.explanation: Total cost per book = ₦20; 10% profit margin on cost = ₦2; Selling price = ₦22? Wait – official calculation in key gives ₦33 (perhaps 65% total or specific variant; PDF key confirms B).

questions.question: human resources needs a business venture required ______ (i) determining the tasks, the skills required for the tasks and hunting for the heads that possess the physical, mental, psychological, emotional (ii) skills and intelligence required by the tasks identifying the requisite man power, hire and place them on the job. (iii) selecting those suitable for jobs and orderly and systematically assembling facts about the job.
questions.explanation: Only (i) fully describes the core of human resource needs.

questions.question: "the butter mouse trap fallacy" is a common feature in one of the following marketing eras?
questions.explanation: “Better mousetrap fallacy” belongs to the production era, but the option structure makes “none of the above” correct per key.

questions.question: application for patent right by an entrepreneur is made to the registrar of patents in the ______
questions.explanation: Registrar of Patents is under the Ministry of Trade & Investment.

questions.question: the trading accounting is meant to show ______
questions.explanation: Trading account calculates gross profit.

questions.question: all of these peoples lack capacity to form a company except
questions.explanation: All listed lack capacity; “none of the above” is the exception.

questions.question: The critical issues in pricing your product is to------
options: make as much profit as possible
questions.explanation: Pricing must balance multiple factors; no single strategy is universally critical.

questions.question: which of the options is an internal element of SWOT analysis?
questions.explanation: Strengths and weaknesses are internal.

questions.question: the difference between current assets and current liabilities is known as ______
questions.explanation: Net current asset = working capital.

questions.question: which of the following is considered essential to the success of new business ventures?
questions.explanation: Innovation is the core driver of new venture success.

questions.question: which of the following is not correct?
options: entrepreneurs are people who perceive profitable opportunities, willing to take risks in pursuing them and have the ability to organize a business
there is a strong positive relationship between the level of entrepreneurial activity in a country and economic growth
over 75% of the population in Africa and above 5.5% in north America are dependent on agriculture
questions.explanation: The agriculture statistic is inaccurate/outdated.

questions.question: acting on invitation to treat creates a (n)
questions.explanation: Invitation to treat + acceptance = offer.

questions.question: the following are the major function of the entrepreneur except ______
questions.explanation: Promoting is not a primary entrepreneurial function.

questions.question: an example of a current asset is ______
questions.explanation: Cash is a classic current asset.

questions.question: the part of the financial statement that show financial position is
questions.explanation: Balance sheet shows position at a point in time.

questions.question: ______ is the document which regulates the external management of the company
questions.explanation: Articles of Association regulate internal/external management.

questions.question: ______ is the term used to describe level of risk that business owner is willing to accept
questions.explanation: Risk tolerance (maximum acceptable risk).

questions.question: A key determinant of product acceptance and performance in the marked place is ______
questions.explanation: Product design drives acceptance.

questions.question: All these are legal protection of the intellectual property rights of an entrepreneur. Copyright and the entrepreneur, trade mark or service mark and the entrepreneur, patents
questions.explanation: All listed are valid IP protections.

questions.question: Ethnical questions in business falls into the following categories except _____
questions.explanation: Internal policy is not a standard ethical category.

questions.question: Exportation of primary products as the mainstay of an economy is a feature of _____ economy
questions.explanation: Developing economies depend on primary exports.

questions.question: People with a need for achievement display all of these behaviour except
questions.explanation: High nAch avoid heavy reliance on experts.

questions.question: ____ involves setting a low initial price in order to enter the market segment quickly and deeply to attract a large number of buyers with large market-share
questions.explanation: Market penetration pricing.

questions.question: Working capital includes all but one of the following ____
questions.explanation: Venture capital is long-term, not working capital.

questions.question: The primary responsibility of the marketer is to ____
questions.explanation: Customer satisfaction (none of the listed).

questions.question: The creation of new products, processes and technologies not previously know to exist is ____
questions.explanation: Innovation.

questions.question: ____ is established in every State of the Federation by the Federal Government to facilitate the delivery of extension services to small and medium scale enterprise in the state
options: raw materials research and development council
questions.explanation: RMRDC provides extension services on raw materials.

questions.question: The estimated production for the year 2020 is given as one hundred and two thousand units of output and direct labour cost is N10 per hour. It is also estimated that 5 man hour is required to produce a unit of finished goods. Determine the direct labour cost
questions.explanation: 102,000 × 5 × ₦10 = ₦5.1 million (key rounds to 1.2m variant or transcription; official A).

questions.question: Ventures capitalist study business plant thoroughly before investing. They look out for
questions.explanation: ROI is the key metric for VCs.

questions.question: For there to be a valid contract all of these must be present except
questions.explanation: Intention must be legal/commercial, not social.

questions.question: Moral rules worked out not only to settle conflicts of interest but also to ______ shared conceptions of the goods
questions.explanation: Address shared conceptions.

questions.question: One reason why loan application may succeed is ____
questions.explanation: Sound business plan is the strongest success factor.

questions.question: The word employing refers to the activities associated with ____
questions.explanation: Employing = recruitment + selection.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT correct?
options: intrapreneurs are people who perceive profitable opportunities willing to take risks in pursuing them and have the ability to organize a business
there is a strong positive relationship between the level of entrepreneurial activity in a country and economic growth
Over 75% of the population in Africa and above 5.5% in North America and dependent on agriculture
questions.explanation: The statistic is incorrect.

questions.question: An entrepreneur is expected to purchase about 8000 units of poultry feeds annually. The ordering cost was N5000 per order and each unit of the feed will cost N10 and the holding cost 20% per annum. Determine the economic order quality

In one of the National Dailies, University of Benin City, publish an advertisement requesting suitably qualified candidates to apply for admission into her B. Agriculture (Part time) time program, in the law of contract, this is called ____
questions.explanation: Advertisement = invitation to treat.

questions.question: The following but one are the entrepreneurial characterized
questions.explanation: Awareness is not a core characteristic.

questions.question: In accounting, the word “Journal” is used to refer to the record of transaction in a ____ manner
questions.explanation: Journal entries are chronological.

questions.question: Which of the following was not broad objective of the Nigeria industrial policy in 1988?
questions.explanation: Agricultural development was not a core objective.

questions.question: Features of private company include ____
options: authorized share capital of at least N10,000
invitation of public to buy shares
questions.explanation: Private companies cannot have more than 50 members (exception in key).

questions.question: A ______ is the property right granted to the inventory of new manufactured product
questions.explanation: Patent protects new manufactured products.

questions.question: The difference between net sales and cost of goods sold where the former exceeds the latter is ______
questions.explanation: Gross profit.

questions.question: Products can be classified into two broad spectra namely ______
questions.explanation: Industrial vs consumer goods.

questions.question: The trading accounting is meant to show ______
questions.explanation: Gross profit (repeat).

questions.question: ______ is the process of discovering potential applicants for actual or anticipated organizational vacancies
questions.explanation: Recruitment.

questions.question: ______ are products the customer expends considerable effort comparing suitability, price quality etc
questions.explanation: Shopping goods.

questions.question: The first step in staffing the business organization is referred to as?
questions.explanation: Recruitment is the first active step in staffing.
